content: Primary treatment: surgical resection. Lobectomy sufficient for low-risk papillary cancers <4cm without extrathyroidal extension; total thyroidectomy for larger tumors, bilateral disease, high-risk features, or when radioactive iodine (RAI) planned. Central neck dissection for clinically involved nodes. Postoperative RAI ablation (I-131) for intermediate/high-risk differentiated thyroid cancer to destroy residual tissue and treat metastases. TSH suppression therapy with levothyroxine reduces recurrence risk in differentiated thyroid cancer (target TSH <0.1 mIU/L for high-risk, 0.1-0.5 for intermediate). External beam radiation for unresectable local disease. Targeted therapies for RAI-refractory disease: lenvatinib, sorafenib (differentiated); vandetanib, cabozantinib (medullary); dabrafenib/trametinib for BRAF-mutated anaplastic. Active surveillance appropriate for very low-risk microcarcinomas (<1cm).
